{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Caching logic with potential stale allowlist checks and incomplete upsampling transformation application could cause inconsistent query results.",
  "circularTests": {

  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Logic Error - Stale Cache",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py",
      "line": 31,
      "title": "Cache key collision risk with project_ids hashing",
      "description": "The cache key uses `hash(tuple(sorted(snuba_params.project_ids)))` which can suffer from hash collisions. Different sets of project IDs could hash to the same value, causing queries for different projects to incorrectly reuse upsampling decisions from unrelated queries. This is a data integrity issue.",
      "suggestion": "Use a deterministic string representation instead: `cache_key = f\"error_upsampling_eligible:{organization.id}:{','.join(map(str, sorted(snuba_params.project_ids)))}\"` to ensure unique cache keys per project combination."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Logic Error - Incomplete Transformation",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_events_stats.py",
      "line": 238,
      "title": "Upsampling transformation applied multiple times with unused initial assignment",
      "description": "The code assigns `final_columns = query_columns` at line 227, but then conditionally reassigns it in three separate locations (lines 238, 275, 295). This pattern is error-prone: if `upsampling_enabled` is True but one of the conditional branches is taken without the transformation, the columns won't be upsampled. Additionally, `final_columns` is transformed within the `if use_rpc:` block but then reassigned again outside it, potentially overwriting the transformation.",
      "suggestion": "Apply the upsampling transformation once at the beginning based on `upsampling_enabled`, then use `final_columns` consistently: `if upsampling_enabled: query_columns = transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling(query_columns)` before all branching logic."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Semantic Error - Inconsistent Cache Behavior",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py",
      "line": 32,
      "title": "Cache returns early without checking dataset eligibility on hit",
      "description": "Line 32 caches and returns `is_eligible and _should_apply_sample_weight_transform(...)` as the result, but on cache hit (line 31), it retrieves only the `is_eligible` value and then applies `_should_apply_sample_weight_transform()` again. This creates inconsistency: the cached boolean represents `is_eligible`, not the final decision, so the second evaluation of `_should_apply_sample_weight_transform()` may produce different results if dataset/request state changes, defeating the caching purpose.",
      "suggestion": "Cache the final boolean result (after both checks), not the intermediate `is_eligible`: `cached_result = cache.get(cache_key); if cached_result is not None: return cached_result` and then `final_result = is_eligible and _should_apply_sample_weight_transform(...); cache.set(cache_key, final_result, 60); return final_result`."
    }
  ]
}
